name: Captieux and Bourideys - loop in the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park
name_original: Captieux et Bourideys  - boucle dans le Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Captieux traverse the diverse landscapes of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park in Gironde, France. The region is characterized by vast pine forests, open moors, and meadows, providing a serene backdrop for cycling. Rolling hills and picturesque countryside, including areas shaped by rivers like the Ciron, offer varied terrain for different skill levels. The area features both paved surfaces and unpaved segments, with elevations generally remaining below 200 meters. Best touring cycling routes around Captieux The most popular touring cycling route is Captieux, Goualade and lac de Taste - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne, an easy 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views within the natural park and passes by Lac de Taste. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Captieux and Bourideys - loop in the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park, a moderate 34.4 miles (55.4 km) path. This route explores more of the regional natural park, featuring varied terrain through forests and open landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Long gravel section – The Squirrel of Captieux loop from Captieux, a 24.3 miles (39.2 km) trail leading through the local countryside and past a unique landmark, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Captieux is defined by pine forests, rolling hills, and tranquil moors. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more demanding paths. The routes in Captieux are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Captieux's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Captieux?
A: There are 8 touring cycling routes around Captieux, offering a variety of experiences through the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park. These routes range from easy to moderate difficulty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Captieux?
A: The touring cycling routes around Captieux feature diverse terrain, including vast pine forests, open moors, and meadows. You'll encounter picturesque countryside with rolling hills, and paths that include both paved surfaces and unpaved segments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6565884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas–Captieux Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, for example, has a gravel surface in its southern section, while the northern part is easily passable for all bike types.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Captieux?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy touring cycling routes around Captieux. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961535399/captieux-goualade-and-lac-de-taste-loop-in-the-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Captieux, Goualade and lac de Taste - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt;, which is 33.3 km long and offers scenic views within the natural park, passing by Lac de Taste.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes in Captieux?
A: Many routes will take you through the serene landscapes of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park, characterized by pine forests and meadows. You can also cycle past &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Squirrel of Captieux&lt;/a&gt;, a unique wooden statue, or enjoy the scenic views around Lac de Taste. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6565884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas–Captieux Greenway&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6565867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Railway Line of Retjons&lt;/a&gt; also offer interesting cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Captieux?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Captieux are circular. For instance, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961535399/captieux-goualade-and-lac-de-taste-loop-in-the-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Captieux, Goualade and lac de Taste - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961506547/captieux-and-bourideys-loop-in-the-landes-de-gascogne-regional-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Captieux and Bourideys - loop in the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, both offering engaging circular journeys through the regional park.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Captieux?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil atmosphere of the pine forests and open landscapes within the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park, as well as the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly cycling routes in Captieux?
A: Yes, Captieux offers routes suitable for families. The easier routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961535399/captieux-goualade-and-lac-de-taste-loop-in-the-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Captieux, Goualade and lac de Taste - loop in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt;, are generally well-suited for family outings. Additionally, the northern part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6565884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas–Captieux Greenway&lt;/a&gt; provides a pleasant, car-free experience that is easily passable for all types of bikes, making it ideal for families.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Captieux?
A: The region around Captieux is enjoyable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, but the pine forests provide shade. It's always a good idea to check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that feature specific cultural or historical points of interest?
A: While the routes primarily focus on natural landscapes, the village of Captieux itself offers traditional Landes de Gascogne architecture and the historic Saint-Martin church. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas Cathedral loop from Bernos-Beaulac&lt;/a&gt; will take you towards the nearby town of Bazas, known for its impressive cathedral.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or drink along the cycling routes?
A: Captieux and surrounding villages offer options for refreshments. For example, the highlight &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Marchand&lt;/a&gt; is listed as a pub in the area. Many routes pass through or near small towns where you can find local cafes and restaurants to refuel during your ride.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the cycling routes in Captieux?
A: Captieux is a rural area, and public transport options might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional train services to nearby towns if you plan to use public transport. Many cyclists prefer to drive to Captieux and park, or start their tours directly from local accommodation.

Q: What are the parking options for cyclists in Captieux?
A: Parking is generally available in Captieux village. For specific routes, you can often find parking areas near trailheads or in the centers of small towns along the routes. It's recommended to look for designated parking spots in Captieux or the starting points of your chosen tour.
tips: Small quiet road, away from traffic.
Typical church of the region, behind which there is a small lake and the Forest of Contemporary Art, where various works are exhibited. The village has an inn that offers daily menus.
Large asphalt road through pine forests, on the border between Landes and Gironde.
